details: Super-test-json@1.2.0 is a trojan disguised as a utility. Its README claims it outputs environment variables as base64-encoded JSON, but the binary entrypoint (bin/cli.js) also recursively renames every.py file found under the current working directory to.py-hacked (rendering them unusable) and executes 'killall python3' to terminate running Python processes. Additionally, the package harvests all environment variables (process.env) and dumps them as base64-encoded output, which can contain API keys, tokens, and credentials. No network exfiltration was observed — the sabotage and credential harvesting are local to the machine where the binary is executed.
